8	/-!
9	# Grassmannian affine charts
10	
11	This file contains the single blueprint-pinned declaration for the affine
12	charts of the Grassmannian scheme: `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart`.
13	
14	For `I ⊆ Fin r` with `#I = d`, the affine chart `U^I` of the Grassmannian
15	`Gr(d, r)` is the spectrum of the polynomial ring in the `d(r-d)` free matrix
16	entries — `Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{q ∉ I}` — which is non-canonically isomorphic
17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
18	
19	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_affine_chart`
20	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).
21	-/
22	
23	set_option autoImplicit false
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
30	
31	/- Blueprint: def:gr_affine_chart (chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex) -/
32	
33	/- Planner note:
34	   Blueprint `def:gr_affine_chart` (Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing together
35	   affine patches"): for `I ⊆ {1..r}` with `#I = d`, `X^I` is the `d×r` matrix
36	   whose `I`-minor is the `d×d` identity and whose other `d(r-d)` entries are
37	   independent indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` over ℤ. The affine chart is
38	   `U^I := Spec ℤ[X^I] = Spec (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)` —
39	   the spectrum of the polynomial ring on the `d(r-d)` free entries; non-canonically
40	   `≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. The prover should build `affineChart` as
41	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` of that `CommRingCat` (the MvPolynomial ring on the
42	   free-entry index type), via
43	   `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` or an equivalent index of cardinality
44	   `d(r-d)`. -/
45	
46	/-- The **affine chart** `U^I` of the Grassmannian `Gr(d, r)` indexed by a
47	`d`-element subset `I : Finset (Fin r)`.
48	
49	Concretely `U^I = Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{p : Fin d, q ∉ I}`, the spectrum of the
50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184	
185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]
198	
199	/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
200	applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`

8	/-!
9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —
27	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
28	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the
31	third `coequifibered` field (Stacks 01LL form of quasi-coherence — strictly
32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under
33	the dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`), and the bodies
34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`.
36	
37	The pinned declarations retained in this (extracted) sub-project are:
38	
39	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
40	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit
41	   `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, and the Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` overlay consumed
42	   by `relativeGluingData`. See `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
43	   Decision 2.
44	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC body)
45	   — the relative-spectrum scheme,
46	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`.
47	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.structureMorphism`
48	   (noncomputable def) — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X`,
49	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`.
50	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
51	   — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is an affine morphism; this is the
52	     substantive consequence of the representability statement of Stacks 01LQ.
53	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
54	   — when `X = Spec R` the relative spectrum is affine (Stacks 01LO).
55	
56	## Note on type expressivity
57	
58	With Lane G landed, `QcohAlgebra X` carries a non-tautological structure
59	(sheaf-of-CommRings + unit). The retained theorem bodies still encode each
60	result by its *intended substantive consequence* (e.g. the universal property
61	is encoded as "the structure morphism is affine", which the representability
62	statement of Stacks 01LQ structurally implies). Following the project rule "Never
63	weaken the type to dodge the proof", the litmus test for each declaration is
64	that unfolding it reveals a non-tautological claim, not `Iso.refl _` or
65	`trivial`. iter-175+ will refine `UniversalProperty` to a
66	`CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` witness once the
67	`O_X`-algebra Hom-set is wired up via the under-category form
68	`Under X.sheaf ⊆ TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X`.

73	Stacks Project, tags 01LL (situation), 01LO (affine-base case), 01LQ (existence +

88	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
89	
90	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
91	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
92	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
93	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
94	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
95	
96	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
97	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
98	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
99	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
100	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.

8	/-!
9	# Graded Hilbert–Serre rationality layer
10	
11	This file houses the `IsRatHilb` toolkit and the `GradedModule` ambient
12	subquotient induction (`subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational`) that establish
13	the rationality of graded Hilbert series (Stacks 00K1). The keystone
14	`gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` shows that for a graded `κ`-module
15	finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` with finite-dimensional components,
16	the Hilbert function is eventually a coefficient sequence of `p · (1 - X)⁻ʳ`.
17	
18	Split from `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean` (iter-021) so the
19	graded-algebra layer and the Quot/Grassmannian-defs layer can be proved in
20	parallel.
21	
22	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GradedHilbertSerre.tex`.
23	-/

27	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — graded Hilbert–Serre rationality -/
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
30	
31	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
32	lemma coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul (F : ℚ⟦X⟧) (n : ℕ) :
33	    ((PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val * F).coeff n
34	      = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), F.coeff k := by
35	  have h1 : (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val = PowerSeries.mk (fun _ => (1 : ℚ)) := by
36	    have := PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_val_succ_eq_mk_add_choose (S := ℚ) (d := 0)
37	    simpa using this
38	  rw [h1, PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk]
39	  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_mk, one_mul]
40	  rw [← Finset.sum_range_reflect (fun k => F.coeff k) (n + 1)]
41	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
42	  intro x _
43	  congr 1
44	
45	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
46	/-- **Antidifference step for rational Hilbert series.** If the first difference
47	`H (n+1) - H n` is, for `n ≫ 0`, the `n`-th coefficient of the rational series
48	`q · (1-X)^{-e}`, then `H` itself is, for `n ≫ 0`, the `n`-th coefficient of
49	`p · (1-X)^{-(e+1)}` for an explicit polynomial `p`. This is the power-series
50	heart of the inductive step in graded Hilbert–Serre (Stacks 00K1). Project-local:
51	Mathlib supplies only the converse extraction `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`. -/
52	lemma rationalHilbert_antidiff
53	    (H δ : ℕ → ℚ) (q : Polynomial ℚ) (e N : ℕ)
54	    (hδ : ∀ n, N < n → δ n = ((q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ e).val).coeff n)
55	    (hH : ∀ n, N < n → H (n + 1) - H n = δ (n + 1)) :
56	    ∃ (p : Polynomial ℚ), ∀ n, N < n →
57	      H n = ((p : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val).coeff n := by
58	  set F : ℚ⟦X⟧ := (q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ e).val with hF
59	  -- Partial-sum identity: the order-`(e+1)` series accumulates the order-`e` coefficients.
60	  have hsum : ∀ m, ((q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val).coeff m
61	      = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (m + 1), F.coeff k := by
62	    intro m
63	    have hmul : (q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val
64	        = (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val * F := by
65	      rw [hF, show (e + 1) = 1 + e from by omega, PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_add, Units.val_mul]
66	      ring
67	    rw [hmul, coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul]
68	  -- Telescoping `H` from its first differences, expressed via `F`.
69	  have hstep : ∀ n, N < n → H (n + 1) - H n = F.coeff (n + 1) := by
70	    intro n hn
71	    rw [hH n hn, hδ (n + 1) (by omega)]
72	  have htel : ∀ j, H (N + 1 + j)
73	      = H (N + 1) + ∑ i ∈ Finset.range j, F.coeff (N + 2 + i) := by
74	    intro j
75	    induction j with
76	    | zero => simp
77	    | succ j ih =>
78	        rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, show N + 2 + j = N + 1 + (j + 1) from by omega]
79	        have hs := hstep (N + 1 + j) (by omega)
80	        rw [show (N + 1 + j) + 1 = N + 1 + (j + 1) from by omega] at hs
81	        linarith [hs, ih]
82	  -- Constant-absorption: a constant function is the order-`(e+1)` coefficient of `C·(1-X)^e`.
83	  have hCconst : ∀ (c : ℚ),
84	      c • (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val
85	        = ((Polynomial.C c * (1 - Polynomial.X) ^ e : Polynomial ℚ) : ℚ⟦X⟧)
86	            * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val := by
87	    intro c
88	    have hkey : (1 - PowerSeries.X : ℚ⟦X⟧) ^ e * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val
89	        = (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val := by
90	      rw [Nat.add_comm e 1]
91	      exact PowerSeries.one_sub_pow_mul_invOneSubPow_val_add_eq_invOneSubPow_val ℚ 1 e
92	    rw [Polynomial.coe_mul, Polynomial.coe_C, Polynomial.coe_pow, Polynomial.coe_sub,
93	      Polynomial.coe_one, Polynomial.coe_X, mul_assoc, hkey, PowerSeries.smul_eq_C_mul]
94	  have hcoeff1 : ∀ m, (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val.coeff m = 1 := by
95	    intro m
96	    have h1 : (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val = PowerSeries.mk (fun _ => (1 : ℚ)) := by
97	      have := PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_val_succ_eq_mk_add_choose (S := ℚ) (d := 0)
98	      simpa using this
99	    rw [h1, PowerSeries.coeff_mk]
100	  -- Assemble the polynomial numerator.

11	/-!
12	# Section graded ring infrastructure, Layer 1: tensor powers of a sheaf of modules
13	
14	This file builds the Mathlib-absent infrastructure of
15	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_SectionGradedRing.tex`, Layer 1
16	(`sec:sgr_tensor_powers`): the tensor product, tensor powers, and twists of
17	sheaves of modules over a scheme `X`, together with the unitor and braiding
18	isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
19	
20	The category `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` of sheaves of modules
21	over a scheme carries **no** monoidal structure in Mathlib (the structure sheaf
22	varies the base ring over opens).  Mathlib *does* supply:
23	
24	* the symmetric monoidal structure on the category of **presheaves** of modules
25	  `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`
26	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Monoidal`), and
27	* the sheafification functor `PresheafOfModules.sheafification`
28	  (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification`).
29	
30	We therefore build the tensor product of sheaves of modules as the sheafification
31	of the objectwise (presheaf) tensor product, following
32	[Stacks, Tag 01CA].
33	
34	## Main definitions
35	
36	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafification` — the scheme-level
37	  sheafification functor `X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules`.
38	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj` (`def:sheafTensorObj`) —
39	  `F ⊗ G := (F.toPresheaf ⊗ G.toPresheaf)^#`.
40	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorPow` (`def:sheafTensorPow`) —
41	  the `m`-th tensor power `L^{⊗m}` of a sheaf of modules.
42	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.moduleTensorPow` (`def:sheafModuleTwist`) —
43	  the `m`-twist `F(m) = F ⊗ L^{⊗m}`.
44	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCounitIso` — the reflective
45	  counit iso `(F.toPresheaf)^# ≅ F`.
46	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjUnitIso`,
47	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjRightUnitor`,
48	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorBraiding` — the left/right unitor and
49	  braiding isomorphisms of the sheaf tensor product.
50	
51	The comparison isomorphism `L^{⊗m} ⊗ L^{⊗m'} ≅ L^{⊗(m+m')}`

63	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}

65	/-- The scheme-level sheafification functor, sending a presheaf of modules over a
66	scheme `X` to its associated sheaf of modules `X.Modules`.  It is the
67	`PresheafOfModules.sheafification` functor for the identity morphism of the
68	underlying presheaf of rings (which is locally bijective).  Non-private because it
69	appears in the statement of `isIso_sheafification_whiskerRight_unit`. -/
70	noncomputable def sheafification : X.PresheafOfModules ⥤ X.Modules :=
71	  PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)
72	
73	/-- The category `X.PresheafOfModules` of presheaves of modules over a scheme,
74	presented in the exact form `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)`
75	for which Mathlib equips it with a symmetric monoidal structure.  This is
76	*definitionally* `X.PresheafOfModules` (since
77	`X.ringCatSheaf.obj = X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat`), so a term of
78	either type is accepted for the other. -/
79	private abbrev MonoidalPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) : Type _ :=
80	  _root_.PresheafOfModules.{u} (X.sheaf.obj ⋙ forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)
81	
82	/-- The tensor product of two sheaves of modules over a scheme, defined as the
83	sheafification of the objectwise tensor product presheaf
84	(Mathlib `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`).  See [Stacks, Tag 01CA]
85	(`def:sheafTensorObj`). -/
86	noncomputable def tensorObj (F G : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
87	  sheafification.obj
88	    (MonoidalCategory.tensorObj (C := MonoidalPresheaf X)
89	      ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj F) ((toPresheafOfModules X).obj G))
90

93	(`lem:moduleUnit_mathlib`). -/
94	private noncomputable abbrev unitModule (X : Scheme.{u}) : X.Modules :=
95	  SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf
96

99	`L^{⊗(m+1)} = L^{⊗m} ⊗ L`.  See [Stacks, Tag 01CU] (`def:sheafTensorPow`). -/

9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
17	```
18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
38	
39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
40	Tag 02KH.
41	-/
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	open Scheme.Modules
50	
51	open scoped TensorProduct
52	
53	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
54	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
87

9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward, global (`H⁰`-as-equalizer) chain
11	
12	This file builds the global ("FBC-B") leg of the `i = 0` flat-base-change package:
13	`H⁰(X, F) = Γ(X, F)` of a quasi-compact, quasi-separated scheme is the equalizer of
14	a *finite* affine cover, and flat base change commutes with that finite equalizer.
15	
16	It is the companion of `AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.FlatBaseChange`, which it imports
17	read-only (using the affine global-sections comparison as a per-term black box).
18	
19	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` (FBC-B section).

22	universe u
23	
24	open CategoryTheory Limits
25	
26	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
27	
28	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — finite affine covers with quasi-compact overlaps -/
29	
30	/-- A quasi-compact scheme admits a *finite* affine open cover; when it is moreover
31	quasi-separated, every pairwise intersection of cover members is quasi-compact.
32	
33	Project-local: packages `isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens` (finite affine
34	subcover of `⊤`) with `quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens` (quasi-compact overlaps)
35	into the single combinatorial input feeding the finite sheaf-condition equalizer of the
36	`H⁰` flat-base-change argument. -/
37	theorem Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact (X : Scheme.{u})
38	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] :
39	    ∃ s : Set X.affineOpens, s.Finite ∧ (⨆ i ∈ s, (i : X.Opens)) = ⊤ ∧
40	      ∀ U ∈ s, ∀ V ∈ s, IsCompact ((U : Set X) ∩ (V : Set X)) := by
41	  obtain ⟨s, hs, he⟩ :=
42	    (isCompact_iff_finite_and_eq_biUnion_affineOpens (U := (⊤ : X.Opens))).mp
43	      (by simpa using isCompact_univ (X := ↥X))
44	  refine ⟨s, hs, he.symm, ?_⟩
45	  intro U _ V _
46	  exact quasiSeparatedSpace_iff_forall_affineOpens.mp ‹_› U V
47	
48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the global sections as a sheaf-condition equalizer -/

50	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in
51	/-- For `M : X.Modules` and any open cover `U : ι → X.Opens`, the sheaf-condition fork
52	of the underlying abelian-group presheaf of `M` is a limit (an equalizer of products):
53	```
54	Γ(M, ⨆ i, U i) ⟶ ∏ i, Γ(M, U i) ⇉ ∏ (i,j), Γ(M, U i ⊓ U j).
55	```
56	This is the equalizer-products form of the sheaf condition specialised to the abelian
57	presheaf `M.presheaf` of a sheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules. Combined with the finite affine cover
58	of `Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact` it computes `Γ(X, M) = Γ(M, ⊤)`
59	as a *finite* equalizer; that finiteness is what the flat-base-change argument needs to
60	commute `- ⊗_A B` past the equalizer.
61	
62	Project-local: packages `M.isSheaf` through Mathlib's
63	`isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts` at the level of `X.Modules`. -/
64	noncomputable def Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules)
65	    {ι : Type u} (U : ι → X.Opens) :
66	    IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U) :=
67	  ((isSheaf_iff_isSheafEqualizerProducts M.presheaf).mp M.isSheaf U).some
68	
69	open TopCat.Presheaf SheafConditionEqualizerProducts in
70	/-- Consolidation of the two preceding supplements: for a quasi-compact, quasi-separated
71	scheme `X` and `M : X.Modules`, there is a *finite* affine open cover `U : ι → X.Opens`
72	(`ι` finite, each `U i` affine, `⨆ i, U i = ⊤`, all pairwise intersections quasi-compact)
73	for which `Γ(X, M) = Γ(M, ⊤)` is the equalizer of the finite sheaf-condition fork of `U`.
74	
75	Project-local: combines `Scheme.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact` (L1) with
76	`Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork` (L2) into the directly-usable "global sections as a
77	finite equalizer" input of the flat-base-change argument. -/
78	theorem Modules.exists_finite_affineCover_isLimit_sheafConditionFork (X : Scheme.{u})
79	    [CompactSpace X] [QuasiSeparatedSpace X] (M : X.Modules) :
80	    ∃ (ι : Type u) (_ : Finite ι) (U : ι → X.Opens),
81	      (∀ i, IsAffineOpen (U i)) ∧ (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
82	      (∀ i j, IsCompact ((U i : Set X) ∩ (U j : Set X))) ∧
83	      Nonempty (IsLimit (fork M.presheaf U)) := by
84	  obtain ⟨s, hs, hcov, hqc⟩ := X.exists_finite_affineCover_inter_isQuasiCompact
85	  have hfin : Finite ↥s := hs.to_subtype
86	  refine ⟨↥s, hfin, fun i => ((i : X.affineOpens) : X.Opens), fun i => i.1.2, ?_, ?_,
87	    ⟨Modules.gammaIsLimitSheafConditionFork M _⟩⟩
88	  · rw [← hcov, iSup_subtype']
89	  · rintro ⟨i, hi⟩ ⟨j, hj⟩
90	    exact hqc i hi j hj

93	
94	This block presents `Γ(X, M)` of a sheaf of modules as a `LinearMap.eqLocus` of two
95	`A`-linear maps over the ground ring `A = Γ(X, ⊤)`, the shape required by

8	/-!
9	# Regrouping isomorphism — pure-tensor-algebra core
10	
11	This file contains `AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_regroup_linearEquiv`, the
12	axiom-clean `R'`-linear equivalence `(A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M ≃ₗ[R'] R' ⊗[R] M`
13	that is the mathematical heart of the section-level flat-base-change mate.
14	
15	It lives in a separate compilation unit so that the `Module A (A ⊗[R] R')`
16	instance diamond (between `Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra` and the
17	`extendScalars`/`restrictScalars` canonical structure) normalises at the
18	import boundary, enabling `FlatBaseChange.lean` to close its
19	`base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` `map_smul'` with the one-liner
20	`exact LinearEquiv.toModuleIso (base_change_regroup_linearEquiv ↑M)`.
21	
22	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_RegroupHelper.tex`.
23	-/
24	
25	set_option autoImplicit false
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	open scoped TensorProduct
30	
31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
32	
33	/-! ## Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate (L4-a) -/
34	
35	/-- **Regrouping isomorphism, pure-tensor-algebra core.** For ring homomorphisms `ψ : R → R'`,
36	`φ : R → A` (giving `A` the `R`-algebra structure) and an `A`-module `M` (with `M` an `R`-module
37	via `φ` and `IsScalarTower R A M`), there is a bundled `R'`-linear isomorphism
38	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M, \]
39	where `R'` acts on the source through the `right`-algebra structure on `A ⊗[R] R'`
40	(`Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra`, i.e. the `includeRight` factor). On the generator it sends
41	`(1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m ↦ r' ⊗ m`; its inverse sends `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`. It carries **no** flatness
42	hypothesis.
43	
44	The construction is exactly the blueprint composite
45	`comm ≪≫ cancelBaseChange ≪≫ comm` (in the `A ⊗_R R'` orientation):
46	\[ (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} M \otimes_A (A \otimes_R R')
47	   \xrightarrow{\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}} M \otimes_R R'
48	   \xrightarrow{\texttt{comm}} R' \otimes_R M, \]
49	assembled at the additive level from Mathlib's `TensorProduct.comm` (×2) and
50	`TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange` (instantiated at the tower `R → A → A` with
51	module `M` and base-change object `R'`), then re-bundled as an `R'`-linear equivalence by verifying
52	`R'`-`map_smul` on generators (`cancelBaseChange_tmul` plus the `rightAlgebra` action
53	`r' • (a ⊗ s) = a ⊗ (r' * s)`). This is the **mathematical heart** of the section-level mate
54	identification (`lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv`); the object-level packaging needed by the
55	chain is `base_change_mate_regroupEquiv` below. Axiom-clean (`propext`, `Quot.sound`).
56	
57	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change", proof, the
58	"boils down to the equality `(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M = R' ⊗_R M`" step. -/
59	noncomputable def base_change_regroup_linearEquiv
60	    {R A R' : Type u} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing R']
61	    [Algebra R A] [Algebra R R'] (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module A M]
62	    [IsScalarTower R A M] :
63	    letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
64	    ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃ₗ[R'] (R' ⊗[R] M) := by
65	  letI : Algebra R' (A ⊗[R] R') := Algebra.TensorProduct.rightAlgebra
66	  -- The underlying additive composite `comm ≫ cancelBaseChange ≫ comm`.
67	  let g : ((A ⊗[R] R') ⊗[A] M) ≃+ (R' ⊗[R] M) :=
68	    (TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M).toAddEquiv.trans
69	      (((TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange R A A M R').toAddEquiv).trans
70	        (TensorProduct.comm R M R').toAddEquiv)
71	  refine { g with map_smul' := ?_ }
72	  intro r' x
73	  simp only [AddEquiv.toFun_eq_coe, RingHom.id_apply]
74	  induction x using TensorProduct.induction_on with
75	  | zero => simp
76	  | add a b ha hb => rw [smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
77	  | tmul t m =>
78	    induction t using TensorProduct.induction_on with
79	    | zero => simp
80	    | add a b ha hb => rw [TensorProduct.add_tmul, smul_add, map_add, map_add, smul_add, ha, hb]
81	    | tmul a s =>
82	      -- `r' • ((a ⊗ s) ⊗ m) = (a ⊗ (r' * s)) ⊗ m` via the `rightAlgebra` action on `A ⊗[R] R'`.
83	      have hsmul : r' • ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m) = (a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m := by
84	        rw [TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
85	        congr 1
86	        rw [Algebra.smul_def, show (algebraMap R' (A ⊗[R] R')) r' = (1 : A) ⊗ₜ[R] r' from rfl,
87	          Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul]
88	        simp
89	      rw [hsmul]
90	      change (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
91	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] (r' * s)) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
92	        = r' • g ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)
93	      show _ = r' • (TensorProduct.comm R M R') (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange
94	          R A A M R' ((TensorProduct.comm A (A ⊗[R] R') M) ((a ⊗ₜ[R] s) ⊗ₜ[A] m)))
95	      simp only [TensorProduct.comm_tmul, TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.cancelBaseChange_tmul,
96	        TensorProduct.smul_tmul']
97	      rw [smul_eq_mul]
98	
99	end AlgebraicGeometry

1928	        -- change, and `exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact` (L3) splices.
1929	        haveI hM'free_base : Module.Free A (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) := inferInstance
1930	        have hM'_free : Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
1931	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)) :=
1932	          (IsLocalizedModule.isBaseChange (Submonoid.powers f) (Localization.Away f)
1933	            (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f)
1934	              (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A))).free
1935	        exact exists_free_localizationAway_of_shortExact A (MvPolynomial (Fin d) A)
1936	          (Fin m → MvPolynomial (Fin d) A) N (N ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) φ
1937	          (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ hφinj (LinearMap.range φ).mkQ_surjective
1938	          (by rw [LinearMap.exact_iff, Submodule.ker_mkQ]) hf0 hf0 hM'_free hTf_free

1942	/-! ## Generic flatness, algebraic form (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic Flatness")
1943	
1944	For a noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
1945	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1946	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.
1947	
1948	The proof splits along the blueprint's decomposition:
1949	
1950	* **Primary route** (provided here, axiom-clean): when `M` is already
1951	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1952	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the
1953	  localised module is a finite vector space hence free, and
1954	  `Module.FinitePresentation.exists_free_localizedModule_powers` descends that
1955	  freeness to a single basic open `D(f)`.
1956	
1957	* **Surviving residue** (`sorry` this iter): when `M` is finite over the
1958	  *finite-type* algebra `B` but not module-finite over `A`, the genuine §4
1959	  dévissage is required — a prime filtration of `M` as a finite `B`-module
1960	  reduces to `M = B/𝔭`, Noether normalisation makes `B_g` finite over the
1961	  polynomial ring `A_g[b₁,…,b_n]`, and induction on the support dimension
1962	  bottoms out at the polynomial-ring core of generic freeness. That core
1963	  (a finite module over `A[X₁,…,X_d]` is generically free) is the precise
1964	  piece Mathlib does not yet supply. -/
1965	
1966	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1967	-- The §4 dévissage assembly in the `B/𝔭` branch (Noether normalisation, the
1968	-- polynomial-ring core, and the ring↔module localisation bridge) drives the deep
1969	-- localisation/quotient instance stack past the default elaboration and
1970	-- instance-search heartbeat limits; both are raised for this declaration.
1971	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 400000 in
1972	/-- **Generic flatness, algebraic form** (Nitsure §4 "Lemma on Generic
1973	Flatness"). Let `A` be a noetherian domain, `B` a finite-type `A`-algebra, and
1974	`M` a finite `B`-module regarded as an `A`-module via the scalar tower
1975	`A → B → M`. Then there exists `f ∈ A`, `f ≠ 0`, such that
1976	`LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M` is free over `Localization.Away f`.
1977	
1978	Blueprint: `thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`. The finite-`A`-module case is the
1979	thin Mathlib wrapper `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite`;
1980	the finite-type residue is the classical §4 dévissage (Noether normalisation +
1981	the polynomial-ring core, both proved above). -/
1982	theorem genericFlatnessAlgebraic
1983	    (A B M : Type u) [CommRing A] [IsDomain A] [IsNoetherianRing A]
1984	    [CommRing B] [Algebra A B] [Algebra.FiniteType A B]
1985	    [AddCommGroup M] [Module B M] [Module.Finite B M]
1986	    [Module A M] [IsScalarTower A B M] :
1987	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧

1987	    ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
1988	      Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
1989	  by_cases hAM : Module.Finite A M
1990	  · -- Primary route: `M` module-finite over `A` ⟹ the finite-module helper.
1991	    haveI := hAM
1992	    exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite A M
1993	  · -- Surviving residue: `M` finite over the finite-type algebra `B` but not
1994	    -- module-finite over `A`. Dévissage over the noetherian ring `B`
1995	    -- (`IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime`) with motive
1996	    -- `P N := ∃ f ≠ 0, Free A_f (N_f)`, each `B`-module `N` carrying its restricted
1997	    -- `A`-action through `algebraMap A B`. The subsingleton obligation discharges
1998	    -- from the torsion base case (L1); the short-exact obligation from the L3
1999	    -- splice; the `N ≅ B/𝔭` obligation is the genuine residual (it needs L4
2000	    -- finiteness + L5, the polynomial-ring core still under construction).
2001	    haveI : IsNoetherianRing B := Algebra.FiniteType.isNoetherianRing A B
2002	    have key : letI : Module A M := Module.compHom M (algebraMap A B)
2003	        ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2004	          Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) M) := by
2005	      refine IsNoetherianRing.induction_on_isQuotientEquivQuotientPrime B
2006	        (inferInstance : Module.Finite B M)
2007	        (motive := fun N _ _ _ => letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2008	          ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧
2009	            Module.Free (Localization.Away f) (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) N))
2010	        ?_ ?_ ?_
2011	      · -- subsingleton `N` ⟹ torsion base case (L1).
2012	        intro N _ _ _ _
2013	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2014	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2015	        refine GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N ?_
2016	        exact LocalizedModule.subsingleton_iff.mpr fun m =>
2017	          ⟨1, Submonoid.one_mem _, by rw [Subsingleton.elim m 0, smul_zero]⟩
2018	      · -- `N ≅ B/𝔭` (domain quotient, finite-type over `A`): the genuine residual.
2019	        -- L4 (`exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial`) and L5
2020	        -- (`exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial`) are now BOTH closed and
2021	        -- axiom-clean, so the remaining work here is pure assembly:
2022	        --   1. Set `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal` (a finite-type `A`-algebra DOMAIN, via
2023	        --      `Ideal.Quotient.isDomain` + `Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective`), and
2024	        --      transport the goal from `N` to `C` along the `B`-linear (hence
2025	        --      `A`-linear) equiv `a`, since freeness through `LocalizedModule` is
2026	        --      preserved by an `A`-linear equivalence.
2027	        --   2. Split on `Subsingleton (C ⊗_A K)` (`K = Frac A`).  If torsion, close
2028	        --      with the L1 leaf `exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A B N`.
2029	        --   3. Otherwise apply L4 to get `g ≠ 0`, `n`, and an injective `A_g`-algebra
2030	        --      map `φ : A_g[X_n] → C_g` with `C_g := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2031	        --      module-finite over `A_g[X_n]` (via `φ.toAlgebra`).  Feed `C_g` (as a
2032	        --      finite `MvPolynomial (Fin n) A_g`-module) to L5 at base `A_g`, obtaining
2033	        --      `h ≠ 0` in `A_g` with `(C_g)_h` free over `(A_g)_h`.
2034	        --   4. Bridge the ring-localisation `C_g = Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g)`
2035	        --      to the module-localisation `LocalizedModule (powers g) C` (an
2036	        --      `IsLocalizedModule`-uniqueness iso), then descend the `A_g`-witness `h`
2037	        --      to a single `f ∈ A` via `free_localizationAway_of_away_tower`.
2038	        -- The only genuinely fiddly step is the ring↔module localisation bridge in (4);
2039	        -- everything else is direct application of the now-closed L1/L4/L5 leaves.
2040	        intro N _ _ _ p a
2041	        letI : Module A N := Module.compHom N (algebraMap A B)
2042	        haveI : IsScalarTower A B N := IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun _ _ => rfl
2043	        classical
2044	        -- Step 1. Pass to the domain quotient `C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal`, a finite-type
2045	        -- `A`-algebra domain. `a` transports the goal between `N` and `C`.
2046	        haveI hp : p.asIdeal.IsPrime := p.isPrime
2047	        haveI : IsDomain (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) := Ideal.Quotient.isDomain p.asIdeal
2048	        haveI : Algebra.FiniteType A (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) :=
2049	          Algebra.FiniteType.of_surjective
2050	            (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ A p.asIdeal) (Ideal.Quotient.mkₐ_surjective A p.asIdeal)
2051	        suffices hC : ∃ f : A, f ≠ 0 ∧ Module.Free (Localization.Away f)
2052	            (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal)) by
2053	          obtain ⟨f, hf, hCfree⟩ := hC
2054	          refine ⟨f, hf, ?_⟩
2055	          haveI := hCfree
2056	          -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) C ≃ₗ[A_f] LocalizedModule (powers f) N`,
2057	          -- the localisation functor applied to `a.symm`, transfers freeness.
2058	          exact Module.Free.of_equiv
2059	            (IsLocalizedModule.mapEquiv (Submonoid.powers f)
2060	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2061	              (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers f) N)
2062	              (Localization.Away f) ((a.restrictScalars A).symm))
2063	        -- Step 2. Torsion / non-torsion split on `C_K = K ⊗_A C`.
2064	        by_cases htors :
2065	            Subsingleton (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal))
2066	        · -- Torsion: the L1 leaf with `B := M := C`.
2067	          exact GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_torsion A
2068	            (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) (B ⧸ p.asIdeal) htors
2069	        · -- Step 3–4. Non-torsion: Noether normalisation (L4) + polynomial core
2070	          -- (L5) + the ring↔module bridge + descent (`away_tower`).
2071	          set C := B ⧸ p.asIdeal with hCdef
2072	          -- `C_K = K ⊗_A C` is nontrivial (the non-torsion hypothesis), via the
2073	          -- identification `LocalizedModule (A⁰) C ≃ K ⊗_A C`.
2074	          haveI : Nontrivial (LocalizedModule (nonZeroDivisors A) C) :=
2075	            not_subsingleton_iff_nontrivial.mp htors
2076	          have hCK : Nontrivial (TensorProduct A (FractionRing A) C) :=
2077	            (LocalizedModule.equivTensorProduct (nonZeroDivisors A) C).injective.nontrivial
2078	          -- Step 3. Noether normalisation: invert `g` so that `C_g` is module-finite
2079	          -- over `A_g[X_1,…,X_n]` through the injective `A_g`-algebra map `φ`.
2080	          obtain ⟨n, g, hg, algBg, φ, hφinj, hφfin, hcompat⟩ :=
2081	            GenericFreeness.exists_localizationAway_finite_mvPolynomial A C hCK
2082	          set Cg := Localization.Away (algebraMap A C g) with hCgdef
2083	          letI := algBg
2084	          letI := φ.toAlgebra
2085	          haveI : Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg := hφfin
2086	          haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away g) :=
2087	            IsLocalization.isDomain_of_le_nonZeroDivisors _
2088	              (Submonoid.powers_le.mpr (mem_nonZeroDivisors_of_ne_zero hg))
2089	          haveI : IsNoetherianRing (Localization.Away g) :=
2090	            IsLocalization.isNoetherianRing (Submonoid.powers g) _ inferInstance
2091	          haveI : IsScalarTower (Localization.Away g)
2092	              (MvPolynomial (Fin n) (Localization.Away g)) Cg :=
2093	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun r => (φ.commutes r).symm
2094	          -- Polynomial core (L5) at base `A_g`: invert `h ∈ A_g` to free `C_g`.
2095	          obtain ⟨h, hh, hCgfree⟩ :=
2096	            GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_polynomial (Localization.Away g) n Cg
2097	          -- Step 4. Bridge `LocalizedModule (powers g) C ≃ₗ[A_g] C_g`. The ambient
2098	          -- `A → C → C_g` tower exhibits `C_g` as the localisation of the `A`-module
2099	          -- `C` at `powers g` (instances inferred); `hcompat` aligns the `A_g`-action
2100	          -- with it, upgrading the `A`-linear bridge to an `A_g`-linear one.
2101	          haveI : IsScalarTower A (Localization.Away g) Cg :=
2102	            IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun x => (hcompat x).symm
2103	          let e0 : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[A] Cg :=
2104	            IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers g)
2105	              (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom A C Cg).toLinearMap
2106	          let e : LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers g) C ≃ₗ[Localization.Away g] Cg :=
2107	            LinearEquiv.extendScalarsOfIsLocalization (Submonoid.powers g)
2108	              (Localization.Away g) e0
2109	          -- (v) lift through `LocalizedModule (powers h) (·)` and transfer L5 freeness.

2111	              (LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers h)

2113	            haveI := hCgfree
2114	            exact Module.Free.of_equiv

2701	/-! ### Project-local Mathlib supplement — G3 flat-locality sub-lemmas
2702	
2703	The flat-locality assembly `lem:gf_flat_locality_assembly` of the blueprint is
2704	synthesised from the four sub-results G3.1–G3.4 below over verified Mathlib
2705	flatness anchors.  Three of them are pure-algebra facts and are formalised here
2706	axiom-clean as stand-alone, reusable statements; the fourth (G3.2,
2707	`lem:gf_stalk_flat_over_base`) and the assembly itself are stated in the
2708	blueprint over *stalks* `F_x` of the sheaf of modules, an abstraction that
2709	Mathlib's `SheafOfModules` API does **not** yet provide (there is no
2710	`SheafOfModules.stalk` / `Scheme.Modules.stalk`).  See the handoff for the
2711	precise missing ingredients connecting these algebraic anchors to the geometric
2712	`genericFlatness` conclusion. -/
2713	
2714	/-- **G3.1 — free patch ⇒ flat patch** (`lem:gf_patch_free_imp_flat`).
2715	In the situation of `gf_flat_locality_assembly` each localized patch section
2716	module `(M_j)_f` is free over `A_f`, hence flat; this records the
2717	freeness-to-flatness passage as the thin wrapper over `Module.Flat.of_free`
2718	that the assembly consumes. Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma. -/
2719	theorem gf_patch_free_imp_flat {R M : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
2720	    [Module.Free R M] : Module.Flat R M :=
2721	  Module.Flat.of_free
2722	
2723	/-- **G3.3 — flatness over a base is local on the source** (`lem:gf_flat_base_local_on_source`).
2724	Let `B` be an `R`-algebra and `N` a `B`-module, viewed as an `R`-module through
2725	the scalar tower `R → B → N`. If for every maximal ideal `Q ⊂ B` the
2726	localization `N_Q = LocalizedModule Q.primeCompl N` is flat over `R`, then `N`
2727	is flat over `R`.  This is the source-locality criterion: flatness over the
2728	fixed base `R` is detected at the maximal ideals of the *source* ring `B`
2729	(distinct from the base-maximal criterion `Module.flat_of_localized_maximal`).
2730	Project-local: instantiates `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` at the
2731	canonical prime localizations of `N`. -/
2732	theorem gf_flat_base_local_on_source {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing B] [Algebra R B]
2733	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
2734	    (h : ∀ (Q : Ideal B) [Q.IsMaximal], Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule Q.primeCompl N)) :
2735	    Module.Flat R N :=
2736	  Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal B _ _ (fun _ _ => LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap _ _) h

2738	/-- **G3.4 — stalk flatness over the local base via transitivity**
2739	(`lem:gf_stalk_flat_localBase`).  Stalk-free algebraic core of the blueprint
2740	sub-lemma: if `R'` is a localization of `R` at a submonoid `S` (so `R'` is flat
2741	over `R`) and `N` is an `R'`-module that is flat over `R'`, then `N` is flat
2742	over `R`.  In the geometric application `R = 𝒪_{S,x}`, `R' = 𝒪_{S,p(y)}` (a
2743	localization of `R` because `p(y)` is a generization of `x`), and `N = F_y`;
2744	this upgrades "`F_y` flat over `𝒪_{S,p(y)}`" to "`F_y` flat over `𝒪_{S,x}`".
2745	Project-local: composes `IsLocalization.flat` with `Module.Flat.trans`. -/
2746	theorem gf_stalk_flat_localBase {R R' N : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing R'] [Algebra R R']
2747	    (S : Submonoid R) [IsLocalization S R'] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R' N] [Module R N]
2748	    [IsScalarTower R R' N] (h : Module.Flat R' N) : Module.Flat R N :=
2749	  haveI : Module.Flat R R' := IsLocalization.flat R' S
2750	  Module.Flat.trans R R' N

2752	/-- **B1.0 — localization commutes with base tensor**
2753	(`lem:gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`).
2754	
2755	Let `R → B` be a ring map, `T ⊆ B` a submonoid, `N` a `B`-module (hence an `R`-module
2756	through the tower `R → B → N`), and `K` an `R`-module.  Then localising `N ⊗[R] K` at the
2757	source submonoid `T` is the same as localising `N` and then tensoring with `K`:
2758	`LocalizedModule T (N ⊗[R] K) ≃ₗ[R] (LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K`.
2759	
2760	The construction uses that `(LocalizedModule T N) ⊗[R] K` is itself a localization of
2761	`N ⊗[R] K` at `T` (`IsLocalizedModule.rTensor`, the base-change identity
2762	`T⁻¹M ⊗[R] N = T⁻¹(M ⊗[R] N)`) and then transports the canonical localization through
2763	uniqueness of localizations (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  It is natural in `K` by construction
2764	(it is the `iso` of a natural transformation).  Project-local: pins the blueprint sub-lemma
2765	feeding `gf_flat_localizedModule_sameBase`. -/
2766	noncomputable def gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm {R B N : Type*} [CommRing R]
2767	    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N]
2768	    (T : Submonoid B) (K : Type*) [AddCommGroup K] [Module R K] :
2769	    LocalizedModule T (TensorProduct R N K) ≃ₗ[R] TensorProduct R (LocalizedModule T N) K :=
2770	  (IsLocalizedModule.iso T
2771	    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R K
2772	      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N))).restrictScalars R

2774	/-- **B1 — localizing the source ring preserves flatness over the base**

2776	
2777	Let `R → B → N` be a scalar tower with `N` a `B`-module, and `T ⊆ B` a submonoid of the
2778	*source* ring `B`.  Viewing `N` as an `R`-module through the tower, if `N` is flat over `R`
2779	then `LocalizedModule T N` is again flat over `R`.

2781	Unlike Mathlib's `Module.Flat.localizedModule` / `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule`, which

2783	the base `R` is untouched.  Proof: flatness is checked by `Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ`;

2786	(cf. `gf_localizedModule_baseChange_tensor_comm`), the `T`-localization of

2788	(`Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap`) and `T`-localization preserves

2790	single genuine Mathlib gap of the flat-locality assembly. -/

2793	    (T : Submonoid B) [Module.Flat R N] :
2794	    Module.Flat R (LocalizedModule T N) := by
2795	  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
2796	  intro P _ _ N₀
2797	  have key := IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (R := R) (S := T)
2798	      (M := N) (M' := LocalizedModule T N) (g := LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap T N)
2799	      (N := N₀) (P := P) (f := N₀.subtype)

2812	/-- **B2.1 — cross-chart basic-open identity** (`lem:gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq`).

2819	theorem gf_crossChart_basicOpen_eq {X : Scheme.{u}} {W Wj : X.Opens}
2820	    (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj))
2821	    (hg : X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wj) (hgbar : X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wj)
2822	    (hmatch : X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g
2823	            = X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar) :
2824	    X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2825	  have h1 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_left : W ⊓ Wj ≤ W)).op g)
2826	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen g := X.basicOpen_res g _
2827	  have h2 : X.basicOpen (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (inf_le_right : W ⊓ Wj ≤ Wj)).op gbar)
2828	          = (W ⊓ Wj) ⊓ X.basicOpen gbar := X.basicOpen_res gbar _
2829	  rw [hmatch, h2, inf_eq_right.mpr hgbar] at h1
2830	  rw [inf_eq_right.mpr hg] at h1
2831	  exact h1.symm

2842	    (g : Γ(X, W)) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj))
2843	    [Module Γ(X, W) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)]
2844	    [IsScalarTower Γ(X, W) Γ(X, X.basicOpen g) Γ(F, X.basicOpen g)]

2861	theorem gf_base_localization_comparison {S : Scheme.{u}}
2862	    {V U : S.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (hU : IsAffineOpen U) (e : U ≤ V) :
2863	    letI : Algebra Γ(S, V) Γ(S, U) := (S.presheaf.map (homOfLE e).op).hom.toAlgebra
2864	    Module.Flat Γ(S, V) Γ(S, U) := by
2865	  -- The identity `𝟙 S` is a flat morphism (it is an open immersion); its `appLE V U` is the
2866	  -- restriction ring map `Γ(S, V) → Γ(S, U)`, so flatness of the morphism gives flatness of the

2869	  have hF : (Scheme.Hom.appLE (𝟙 S) V U e').hom.Flat :=
2870	    Flat.flat_appLE (𝟙 S) hV hU e'

2872	    rw [Scheme.Hom.appLE, Scheme.Hom.id_app]
2873	    exact Category.id_comp _
2874	  rw [happ] at hF
2875	  exact hF

2877	/-- **Per-point common basic open** (geometric basis lemma feeding B2.4).
2878	For two affine opens `W, Wᵢ` of a scheme `X` and a point `x ∈ W ⊓ Wᵢ`, there is a section
2879	`g ∈ Γ(X, W)` and a partner `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)` with `x ∈ D(g)`, both basic opens inside the overlap
2880	`W ⊓ Wᵢ`, that cut out the *same* open `X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen ḡ`.  This is the statement
2881	that the opens simultaneously basic in `W` and in `Wᵢ` form a basis of `W ∩ Wᵢ`.  Construction:
2882	pick a basic open `D(b)` of `Wᵢ` inside the overlap around `x` (`IsAffineOpen.exists_basicOpen_le`
2883	in chart `Wᵢ`), then a basic open `D(g)` of `W` inside `D(b)` (in chart `W`); finally realise
2884	`D(g)` back as a basic open `D(ḡ)` of `Wᵢ` by writing the restriction `g|_{D(b)}` through the

2891	(which is all B2.2/the assembly consume; `D(g) = D(ḡ)` makes `Γ(F, D(g))` one group).  The
2892	conclusion here is therefore stated as the basic-open equality, not the restriction equality.

2924	    {ι : Type*} (Wj : ι → X.Opens) (hWj : ∀ i, IsAffineOpen (Wj i))
2925	    (hcov : W ≤ ⨆ i, Wj i) :
2926	    ∃ (t : Finset Γ(X, W)), Ideal.span (t : Set Γ(X, W)) = ⊤ ∧
2927	      ∀ g ∈ t, ∃ (i : ι) (gbar : Γ(X, Wj i)),
2928	        X.basicOpen g ≤ W ⊓ Wj i ∧ X.basicOpen gbar ≤ W ⊓ Wj i ∧
2929	        X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2930	  classical
2931	  -- For each point of `W`, choose a chart `Wᵢ ∋ x` and a common basic open `D(g) ∋ x` cut out
2932	  -- also by a partner `ḡ ∈ Γ(X, Wᵢ)` (the geometric basis lemma `gf_common_basicOpen_basis`).

2935	      X.basicOpen g = X.basicOpen gbar := by
2936	    intro y
2937	    obtain ⟨i, hi⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov y.2)
2938	    obtain ⟨g, gbar, hmem, hgle, hgble, hbo⟩ :=
2939	      gf_common_basicOpen_basis hW (hWj i) (y : X) y.2 hi
2940	    exact ⟨g, i, gbar, hmem, hgle, hgble, hbo⟩
2941	  choose g idx gbar hmem hgle hgble hbo using hpt
2942	  -- The basic opens `D(g y)` cover `W`, so the `g y` span the unit ideal of `Γ(X, W)`.
2943	  have hspan : Ideal.span (Set.range g) = ⊤ := by
2944	    rw [← hW.self_le_iSup_basicOpen_iff]
2945	    intro y hy
2946	    rw [iSup_range', TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
2947	    exact ⟨_, hmem ⟨y, hy⟩⟩
2948	  obtain ⟨t, ht_sub, ht_span⟩ := (Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite _).mp hspan
2949	  refine ⟨t, ht_span, ?_⟩
2950	  intro h hh
2951	  obtain ⟨y, rfl⟩ := ht_sub hh
2952	  exact ⟨idx y, gbar y, hgle y, hgble y, hbo y⟩
